name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing a post-expansion partial-profit runner structure that keeps a core position for persistence. The edge should come from active de-risking after expansion rather than all-or-nothing exits.
invalidation_condition: Abort if expansions fade too quickly, if persistence and hold filters conflict, or if the runner cannot add enough value after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: An impulse expansion continues for a few bars when the first burst is not fully faded, allowing partial profit plus a retained runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in mostly choppy regimes, structurally different assets can produce occasional burst persistence. Partial profit plus runner management is the cleanest way to test whether those bursts are monetizable.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is a useful structurally different lane because it stress-tests whether the runner logic survives a more volatile personality than ETH or BTC.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If expansions resolve as one-bar events instead of multi-bar persistence, the runner logic will overstay.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but runner capture is weak, shorten the runner and tighten the early de-risk before altering the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; expansion and persistence are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by moderate expansion thresholds rather than extreme breakout requirements
